BD BD 2026 Guidebook March 12, 2026

A practical operating manual for business developers in web3, with a global view and a Korea-led edge.

  2. 02 Global Market Map Where the real money, urgency, and buyer budgets are forming.
  3. 03 Business Models That Matter The hottest and most durable BMs in web3 right now.
  4. 04 Regulation And Market Structure What a BD must know to avoid wasting cycles and to win regulated deals.
  5. 05 Korea-Led Strategy How to think from Korea outward instead of copying U.S. narratives.
  6. 06 BD Operator Manual How to run pipeline, partnerships, discovery, and close motion.

  8. 08 Scorecards And Templates Reusable frameworks for partner qualification and account planning.
  9. 09 Career Positioning How to position yourself for senior BD and strategic roles.
  10. 10 Sources And Ongoing Reading A source map and reading stack anchored to current official references.
Open printable full book

Private offline study guide

12-Week Study Curriculum

A detailed study guide with weekly outputs and learning goals.

12-Week Study Curriculum

How to study

Study in three modes every week:

  • concept mode: understand the market and frameworks
  • operating mode: turn concepts into account strategy and deal logic
  • output mode: write, model, or present something so the knowledge becomes usable

You should produce artifacts weekly.

That is how you convert knowledge into career leverage.

Weekly cadence

  • Monday: market reading and note consolidation
  • Tuesday: one deep concept topic
  • Wednesday: one company or protocol case study
  • Thursday: one account strategy or business model teardown
  • Friday: one memo or LinkedIn-quality note
  • Saturday: discussion, review, or language practice for client-facing communication

Week 1: Industry map

Learn:

  • market structure of crypto and digital assets
  • who the major buyer types are
  • why stablecoins, tokenization, and compliance matter now

Deliverable:

  • a one-page market map with your top three opportunity areas

Week 2: Stablecoin economics

Learn:

  • how stablecoin settlement works
  • corridor economics
  • float, fees, spreads, and treasury value
  • why enterprises care

Deliverable:

  • a memo comparing traditional cross-border settlement with a stablecoin-based workflow

Week 3: Payments market structure

Learn:

  • issuers, PSPs, acquirers, banks, wallets, and settlement providers
  • what part of the stack your target company can realistically own

Deliverable:

  • a market map of potential partners and competitors in one payment corridor

Week 4: Tokenization and STO

Learn:

  • issuance logic
  • servicing
  • custody
  • transfer restrictions
  • why tokenization often fails commercially

Deliverable:

  • a two-page concept note for one Korean tokenization use case

Week 5: Regulation fundamentals

Learn:

  • Korea, U.S., EU, Hong Kong, and Singapore at a high level
  • licensing perimeter
  • AML and travel rule basics
  • securities risk

Deliverable:

  • a country comparison sheet with practical BD implications

Week 6: Custody and controls

Learn:

  • custody models
  • policy engines
  • wallet governance
  • operational risk

Deliverable:

  • a checklist for enterprise digital asset onboarding

Week 7: Compliance stack

Learn:

  • KYC and KYB
  • wallet screening
  • transaction monitoring
  • sanctions exposure
  • reporting workflows

Deliverable:

  • a partner evaluation sheet for a compliance vendor

Week 8: Korea-led thesis building

Learn:

  • Korea-specific opportunity sectors
  • banking realities
  • how to localize value proposition
  • which domestic advantages are exportable

Deliverable:

  • your Korea-outward thesis in one page

Week 9: BD discovery and account planning

Learn:

  • how to run discovery
  • how to qualify opportunities
  • who the internal champions are

Deliverable:

  • one target account brief with proposed discovery questions

Week 10: Deal design

Learn:

  • pilot scoping
  • legal and operational assumptions
  • pricing logic
  • expansion path

Deliverable:

  • a sample pilot proposal with success metrics

Week 11: Thought leadership

Learn:

  • how to write clear market notes
  • how to explain web3 to enterprise buyers in plain language
  • how to show judgment without hype

Deliverable:

  • one publishable article or long post

Week 12: Career packaging

Learn:

  • how to present your thesis
  • how to document wins and case studies
  • how to speak like an operator, not a commentator

Deliverable:

  • a BD portfolio packet with your market thesis, target sectors, and one case study

Detailed topic guide

If you want depth, organize your note system around these topic buckets.

A. Stablecoins

Know:

  • issuer models
  • reserve model basics
  • settlement flow
  • wallet and custody choices
  • on-off ramp design
  • corridor economics
  • merchant and treasury use cases

Be able to explain:

  • why stablecoins matter even to users who do not care about crypto
  • why bank integration and compliance are part of the product

B. Tokenization

Know:

  • legal wrapper logic
  • difference between a token and a security claim
  • transfer restrictions
  • servicing and reporting
  • secondary trading challenges

Be able to explain:

  • when tokenization creates actual value and when it is mostly cosmetic

C. Compliance and regulation

Know:

  • KYC and KYB basics
  • travel rule
  • sanctions and wallet screening
  • licensing perimeter
  • securities risk

Be able to explain:

  • how regulatory uncertainty changes sales cycle design

D. Payments and treasury

Know:

  • settlement timing
  • liquidity needs
  • pre-funding
  • FX spread
  • reconciliation
  • treasury workflow

Be able to explain:

  • where the actual savings come from in a cross-border workflow

E. Enterprise sales

Know:

  • procurement logic
  • security review
  • legal review
  • executive sponsorship
  • pilot design

Be able to explain:

  • why enterprise deals fail and how to de-risk them early

Your reading discipline

Do not just consume crypto media.

Read across:

  • official regulator releases
  • bank and payment company announcements
  • issuer and custody documentation
  • serious market structure analysis
  • earnings calls and investor letters from adjacent fintech companies

The rule

Every week, convert knowledge into one of:

  • memo
  • presentation
  • account brief
  • market map
  • pitch rewrite

Otherwise the learning will remain passive.
